mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses 5.0% upward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving corporate output capacity and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing periods yield negative 5d forward returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic expansion and supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window despite consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising real personal income indicates wealth concentration without broad economic expansion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDP-income divergence metric shows no predictive power for 60d returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases predict positive 5d returns in more than 55% of instances.
